settings.name: Last on, first off
settings.description: Put your jewellery on after dressing, and remove it before bathing, swimming, or exercising. Water, steam, and sweat are the quickest way to dull a finish.

settings.name: Keep away from chemicals
settings.description: Avoid contact with perfumes, lotions, and hairspray. Apply them first and let them settle before you put your pieces on.

settings.name: Wipe after every wear
settings.description: Gently wipe with a soft, dry cloth after each wear to remove moisture and oils. This one habit does more than anything else to keep the shine.

settings.name: Store airtight, in the dark
settings.description: Keep each piece in the airtight pouch provided, away from direct sunlight and humidity. Sealed and shaded, silver stays brighter for far longer.

settings.name: Keep pieces separate
settings.description: Store pieces individually to prevent scratching. Chains, stones, and polished surfaces mark one another when they sit together.

settings.title: Wipe with a soft cloth
settings.body: Use the polishing cloth provided, or any lint-free soft cloth. Buff gently in straight lines to lift oils and light tarnish.

settings.title: For a deeper clean
settings.body: Dip in lukewarm water with a drop of mild soap, wipe softly with your fingertips, then rinse and pat completely dry. Silver and gold-plated pieces only — avoid on kundan, pearls, and delicate stones.

settings.title: Dry thoroughly
settings.body: Moisture is the enemy of shine. Dry every surface and setting fully before returning the piece to its airtight pouch.

settings.title: Never use harsh cleaners
settings.body: Avoid toothpaste, chemical dips, and abrasive brushes — they strip plating and scratch stones. When in doubt, send it to us for a professional polish.

settings.name: Sterling silver
settings.body: 925 silver naturally darkens over time — this patina is normal, not a fault. A soft polishing cloth restores its brightness in moments; store airtight to slow tarnish.

settings.name: Kundan & Jadau
settings.body: Uncut stones are set with natural lac, so keep these pieces away from water and heat. Never submerge them — wipe only the metal gently with a dry cloth.

settings.name: Gold-plated
settings.body: Plating is a fine surface layer. Handle gently, keep it away from friction and chemicals, and clean only with a dry cloth to preserve the finish for years.

settings.hero_sub: Your Rangtarini piece is built to last — with a little care, it will stay luminous for years. A few gentle habits keep the metal bright, the stones clear, and the finish as beautiful as the day it left our bench.
settings.promise_body: Each Rangtarini piece is made by hand to be worn and loved, not locked away. Fine jewellery only asks for a little attention in return — keep it away from moisture and chemicals, store it gently, and it will hold its glow for generations.
settings.ess_meta:
Five simple habits
A minute a day
settings.avoid_body: Everyday products and elements are the most common cause of tarnish and clouded stones. A moment's care avoiding these keeps your piece looking new.
settings.avoid_chips:
Perfume
Lotion & Cream
Hairspray
Chlorine
Saltwater
Sweat
Household Cleaners
Humidity
Direct Sunlight
settings.clean_body: If a piece looks a little tired, a gentle clean at home brings it back. Always be soft — never scrub, and keep water away from strung kundan and glued settings.
settings.mats_body: Each of our materials wears a little differently. Here's how to keep every kind of Rangtarini piece at its best.
settings.quickref_items:
Soft, dry cloth only — never scrub or use chemicals
Airtight pouch, away from sunlight and humidity
Store each piece separately to prevent scratches
settings.cta_body: Loved a piece a little too well? Our bench in Udaipur re-polishes, re-plates, and restrings Rangtarini jewellery so it looks its best for years to come. Reach out and we'll guide you.
